candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue label and matches Net sales values.
Also a direct top-line sales label; useful as alternate label across years.
Operating revenue component, but not the primary total revenue line.
Explicitly non-revenue, so not appropriate for REVT.

variables.notes: Using the reported top-line revenue labels as a priority list; do not sum them.
No clear cost of goods sold / cost of sales line appears on the provided Income Statement. This looks like a service-style statement with operating expense components instead of a distinct COGS row.

candidates.why: Core overhead component and a standard SG&A-type operating expense for service businesses.
Likely selling/admin/other operating overhead bucket; appropriate SG&A component.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A subtotal is present, so selected the best overhead component rows. Excluded depreciation/amortization, acquisition-related items, totals, and balancing rows.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Capitalized Research & Development Expenses - Supplemental
candidates.why: R&D-related, but this is amortization of previously capitalized R&D, not current-period R&D expense.
Balance sheet asset, not income statement R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Capitalized expenditures for program Development , Gross
candidates.why: Gross balance sheet asset, not R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Acc Amortization Capitalized expenditures for program Development
candidates.why: Accumulated amortization balance sheet contra-asset, not current R&D expense.

candidates.row_label: Capitalised expenditures for program development - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing row, not R&D expense.

variables.notes: No clear current-period R&D expense line is reported. The available R&D-related rows are capitalization/amortization or balance-sheet asset lines, which should not be used for XRD.

candidates.why: Broad finance-cost line; usable only as fallback because it includes non-interest items.
Best direct match for interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Specific interest expense subtype; useful fallback for years where broader interest line is absent.
Semantically relevant but appears unpopulated in preview and overlaps with lease-interest labels.
Finance-related but not specifically interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental version of lease interest; lower priority than primary statement labels.
Direct interest-expense label, though not populated in preview.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Priority list uses the most specific interest-expense labels first, with Financial Expenses as a fallback only where interest is not separately disclosed.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to Parent Company shareholders
candidates.why: Best book equity measure because it excludes minority interest and matches the rule to avoid double counting with MIB.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders without a controlling influence
candidates.why: This is non-controlling interest, so it belongs in MIB, not BE.
Usable only if no parent/owner equity line exists; here a parent-only equity line does exist.
Entire balance sheet total, not equity.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to Parent Company shareholders

variables.notes: Selected parent shareholders' equity to exclude non-controlling interests from BE.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, not the preferred balance sheet minority interest stock measure.
Direct balance sheet minority interest / non-controlling interest line.

variables.notes: Using the balance sheet minority interest line as the preferred non-controlling interest measure.
